evidence: "Great family movie - humorous and moving dialog for all family members to appreciate."
"This is an especially engaging movie as it doesn't shove religion or spirituality down your throat but unfolds in a moving and honest fashion."
"It's so well done, such attention to detail - more than one thing going on almost all the time - to move the story forward."
"Some viewers complaining the Peck character showing no grief for his family is unrealistic but have they ever had to keep family (or staff) on point inspite of devastating circumstances?"
"It's about time travel after all. Also the reflective moments, believability in acting, save for one or two, is high quality."
"I tried to watch this movie three times and can not finish as it is way too 'out there' in terms of 'where the heck is this movie going!?"
"It's well directed, a little gem that should be made more easily available/affordable."
"Outstanding story telling that defies American film-making schlock. Great dialog, editing, lighting and especially acting pull this into gem-status."
"I think this season was the best of all. Great dialog, directing and, of course, acting."
"Great idea to sell these two movies together. Having two Hitchcock films together..."
"Beautifully done. Haven't LOL'd in a long time. And GREAT GREAT extras. Enjoy!"
"This is one of THE most well thought out, well written imaginings of how people, real people, might play out this type of catastrophy..."
"A lotta folks are missing subtleties, or not praising the writing and performing of, individual relationships that unfold from the start of the picture to the end..."
"This is an especially engaging movie as it doesn't shove religion or spirituality down your throat but unfolds in a moving and honest fashion."

summary: The user shows a strong preference for family-oriented and dramatic films with complex narratives and high production quality. They appreciate emotional depth and sensory experiences in storytelling, while being less loyal to specific creators or brands.
